title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after a historic four-night concert run.
Kahan joined artists like Billy Joel and Paul McCartney as the fourth inductee honored at Fenway Park.
The concerts featured appearances from Boston sports figures and coincided with 'Noah Kahan Day', as proclaimed by Gov. Maura Healey.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 as part of a $45 million settlement with Cash App over deceptive practices.
Block Inc., owner of Cash App, allegedly misled users about its safety and failed to prevent rising fraud.
Settlement mandates improved security, customer support, and consumer education to prevent future fraud incidents.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly operated a police boat on Lake Mascuppic with emergency lights activated.
The man faces a potential charge of impersonating an officer after reportedly using the boat to enforce boating rules.
Tyngsborough police plan to issue a court summons; the police boat was removed to prevent further incidents.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels sailed into Boston for the six-day Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail featuring ships from over 20 countries arriving from New York.
Thousands of visitors gathered in Boston to witness the ships, celebrating the final stop of the Sail250 tour.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox drafted shortstop Jake Schaffner with the No. 20 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner played for the University of North Carolina, hitting .356 with 6 home runs and 50 RBI in 68 games.
Defensively, Schaffner recorded a .987 fielding percentage with just three errors, but may move to second base in pro baseball.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail 2026 featured historic ships led by the USS Constitution.
Spectators from Castle Island to Piers Park observed the parade, honoring historical maritime contributions.
The event included notable vessels like the Grand Banks replica schooner Bluenose Two from Canada.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts before being demoted to Triple-A Worcester.
Jayson Stark of The Athletic awarded Bello the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' for his poor performance.
Bello excelled in relief, with a 0.71 ERA, but was still sent to Triple-A by the Red Sox.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox are in the midst of a seven-game winning streak, close to a wildcard spot.
Boston's trade deadline approach remains uncertain with recent improvements amid prior sell-off plans.
Craig Breslow's job may depend on Red Sox's decision to buy or sell by the deadline.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert featured hit songs and new tracks, emphasizing themes of resilience and empowerment.
The setlist included hits like 'Hips Don't Lie' and new songs from her Grammy-winning album 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran.'

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester involve converting historic sites and developing underused properties.
The Housing Development Incentive Program increased funding to $30 million annually, supporting housing improvements in Gateway Cities.
